rationale: This example shows the oxidation of a simple primary alcohol, 1-butanol, to the corresponding aldehyde, butanal. This is a classic textbook transformation for the Pfitzner-Moffatt oxidation. The reagents shown are dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), dicyclohexylcarbodiimide (DCC), and pyridinium hydrochloride as the mild acid catalyst, which are the defining components of this reaction. The use of a simple, acyclic, aliphatic substrate avoids potential side reactions and focuses on the core functional group transformation.
collision_avoidance_note: The substrate is a simple primary alcohol, avoiding complex structures, arenes, or other reactive functional groups that could overlap with other named reactions. The core transformation is a straightforward alcohol-to-aldehyde oxidation, which is specific to this class of reactions when these particular reagents (DMSO/carbodiimide/acid) are specified. This avoids collision with other oxidation reactions like Swern (which uses oxalyl chloride) or Corey-Kim (which uses NCS).
